question: X who was closing his books on 31.3.20X2 failed to take the actual stock which he did only on 9th April, 20X2, when it was ascertained by him to be worth ₹ 2,50,000. It was found that sales are entered in the sales book on the same day of dispatch and return inwards in the returns book as and when the goods are received back. Purchases are entered in the purchases day book once the invoices are received. It was found that sales between 31.3.20X2 and 9.4.20X2 as per the sales day book are ₹ 17,200. Purchases between 31.3.20X2 and 9.4.20X2 as per purchases day book are ₹ 1,200, out of these goods amounting to ₹ 500 were not received until after the stock was taken. Goods invoiced during the month of March, 20X2 but goods received only on 4th April, 20X2 amounted to ₹ 1,000. Rate of gross profit is 33-1/3% on cost. Ascertain the value of physical stock as on 31.3.20X2.
answer:
| Statement of Valuation of Physical Stock as on 31st March, 20X2 |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| Value of stock as on 9th April, 20X2                            | ₹ 2,50,000 |
| Add: Cost of sales during the intervening period                |            |
| Sales made between 31.3.20X2 and 9.4.20X2                       | ₹ 17,200   |
| Less: Gross profit @25% on sales                                | (4,300)    |
|                                                                 | 12,900     |
|                                                                 | 2,62,900   |
| Less: Purchases actually received during the intervening period:|            |
| Purchases from 1.4.20X2 to 9.4.20X2                             | 1,200      |
| Less: Goods not received upto 9.4.20X2                          | (500)      |
|                                                                 | 700        |
|                                                                 | 2,62,200   |
| Less: Purchases during March, 20X2 received on 4.4.20X2         | 1,000      |
| Value of physical stock as on 31.3.20X2                         | 2,61,200   |
question_cleaned: X who was closing his books on 31.3.20X2 failed to take the actual stock which he did only on 9th April, 20X2, when it was ascertained by him to be worth ₹ 2,50,000. It was found that sales are entered in the sales book on the same day of dispatch and return inwards in the returns book as and when the goods are received back. Purchases are entered in the purchases day book once the invoices are received. It was found that sales between 31.3.20X2 and 9.4.20X2 as per the sales day book are ₹ 17,200. Purchases between 31.3.20X2 and 9.4.20X2 as per purchases day book are ₹ 1,200, out of these goods amounting to ₹ 500 were not received until after the stock was taken. Goods invoiced during the month of March, 20X2 but goods received only on 4th April, 20X2 amounted to ₹ 1,000. Rate of gross profit is 33-1/3% on cost. Ascertain the value of physical stock as on 31.3.20X2.

question:
Raj Ltd. prepared their accounts financial year ended on 31st March 20X2. Due to unavoidable circumstances actual stock has been taken on 10th April 20X2, when it was ascertained at ₹ 5,00,000. It has been found that:
(i) Sales are entered in the Sales Book on the day of dispatch and return inwards in the Returns Inward Book on the day of the goods received back.
(ii) Purchases are entered in the Purchase Book on the day the Invoices are received.
(iii) Sales between 1st April 20X2 to 9th April 20X2 amounting to ₹ 80,000 as per Sales Day Book.
(iv) Free samples for business promotion issued during 1st April 20X2 to 9th April 20X2 amounting to ₹ 16,000 at cost.
(v) Purchases during 1st April 20X2 to 9th April 20X2 amounting to ₹ 40,000 but goods amount to ₹ 8,000 not received till the date of stock taking.
(vi) Invoices for goods purchased amounting to ₹ 80,000 were entered on 28th March 20X2 but the goods were not included in stock.

Rate of Gross Profit is 25% on cost. Ascertain the value of Stock as on 31st March, 20X2.
answer:
Statement of Valuation of Physical Stock as on 31st March, 20X2

|                                                         | ₹         |
|---------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| Value of stock as on 10th April, 20X2                   | 5,00,000  |
| Add: Cost of sales during the intervening period        |           |
| Sales made between 1.4.20X2 and 9.4.20X2                | 80,000    |
| Less: Gross profit @20% on sales                        | (16,000)  |
| Free sample                                             | 16,000    |
|                                                         | 5,80,000  |
| Less: Purchases actually received during the intervening period: |           |
| Purchases from 1.4.20X2 to 9.4.20X2                     | 40,000    |
| Less: Goods not received upto 9.4.20X2                  | (8,000)   |
|                                                         | 5,48,000  |
| Add: Purchases during March, 20X2 but not recorded in stock | 80,000    |
| Value of physical stock as on 31.3.20X2                 | 6,28,000  |
question_cleaned:
Raj Ltd. prepared their accounts financial year ended on 31st March 20X2. Due to unavoidable circumstances actual stock has been taken on 10th April 20X2, when it was ascertained at ₹ 5,00,000. It has been found that:
(i) Sales are entered in the Sales Book on the day of dispatch and return inwards in the Returns Inward Book on the day of the goods received back.
(ii) Purchases are entered in the Purchase Book on the day the Invoices are received.
(iii) Sales between 1st April 20X2 to 9th April 20X2 amounting to ₹ 80,000 as per Sales Day Book.
(iv) Free samples for business promotion issued during 1st April 20X2 to 9th April 20X2 amounting to ₹ 16,000 at cost.
(v) Purchases during 1st April 20X2 to 9th April 20X2 amounting to ₹ 40,000 but goods amount to ₹ 8,000 not received till the date of stock taking.
(vi) Invoices for goods purchased amounting to ₹ 80,000 were entered on 28th March 20X2 but the goods were not included in stock.

Rate of Gross Profit is 25% on cost. Ascertain the value of Stock as on 31st March, 20X2.

question: Write short notes on the following: Periodic Inventory System Vs Perpetual Inventory System
answer:
| Periodic Inventory System                          | Perpetual Inventory System                           |
|----------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 1. This system is based on physical verification.  | It is based on book records.                         |
| 2. This system provides information about inventory and cost of goods sold at a particular date | It provides continuous information about inventory and cost of sales. |
| 3. This system determines inventory and takes cost of goods sold as residual figure. | It directly determines cost of goods sold and computes inventory as balancing figure. |
| 4. Cost of goods sold includes loss of goods as goods not in inventory are assumed to be sold. | Closing inventory includes loss of goods as all unsold goods are assumed to be in inventory |
| 5. Under this method, inventory control is not possible. | Inventory control can be exercised under this system. |
| 6. This system is simple and less expensive.       | It is costlier method.                               |
| 7. Periodic system requires closure of business for counting of inventory. | Inventory can be determined without affecting the operations of the business. |
question_cleaned: Write short notes on the following: Periodic Inventory System Vs Perpetual Inventory System

question:
The following details are available of raw material of a manufacturing unit:

| Date     | Particulars            | Units | Rate per unit |
|----------|------------------------|-------|---------------|
| 1-5-20X2 | Opening Inventory      | 100   | @ ₹ 15 per unit |
| 2-5-20X2 | Purchases              | 300   | @ ₹ 18 per unit |
| 5-5-20X2 | Issued for consumption | 250   | units          |
| 16-5-20X2| Purchases              | 500   | @ ₹ 21 per unit |
| 21-5-20X2| Issued for consumption | 100   | units          |
| 25-5-20X2| Issued for consumption | 450   | units          |

The manufacturer also incurred the following expenses:
- Freight ₹ 300 and unloading charges of ₹ 150 at the time of every purchase respectively.
- Warehouse rent of ₹ 2,000 per month.



- Administrative Expenses of ₹ 1,500 per month.  
You are required to find out the value of inventory as on May 31, 20X2 if the company follows:  
(a) Weighted Average method for inventory valuation.  
(b) First in First Out method for inventory valuation.
answer:
(a) Weighted Average basis  

| Date    | Receipts          | Calculation of the value of Inventory as on 31-5-20X2 | Issues          | Balance          |
|---------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|------------------|
|         | Units | Rate | Amount (₹) | Units | Rate | Amount | Units | Rate | Amount (₹) |
| 1-5-X2  | Balance           |                                                   |                 | 100   | 15   | 1,500  |
| 2-5-X2  | 300   | 19.5 | 5,850     |                                           |                 | 400   | 18.375 | 7,350  |
| 5-5-X2  |                   |                                                   | 250   | 18.375 | 4593.75 | 150   | 18.375 | 2756.25 |
| 16-5-X2 | 500   | 21.9 | 10,950    |                                           |                 | 650   | 21.09  | 13,708.5 |
| 21-5-X2 |                   |                                                   | 100   | 21.09  | 2,109   | 550   | 21.09  | 11,599.5 |
| 25-5-X2 |                   |                                                   | 450   | 21.09  | 9,490.5 | 100   | 21.09  | 2,109   |

Therefore, the value of Inventory is as follows:  
Value as per Weighted Method as on 31-5-2025: 100 units @ ₹ 21.09 = ₹ 2,109  

(b) First-in-First out basis  

| Date    | Receipts          | Calculation of the value of Inventory as on 31-5-20X2 | Issues          | Balance          |
|---------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|------------------|
|         | Units | Rate | Amount (₹) | Units | Rate | Amount | Units | Rate | Amount (₹) |
| 1-5-X2  | Balance           |                                                   |                 | 100   | 15   | 1,500  |
| 2-5-X2  | 300   | 19.5 | 5,850     |                                           |                 | 100   | 15   | 1,500  |
|         |                   |                                                   |                 | 300   | 19.5  | 5,850  |
| 5-5-X2  |                   |                                                   | 100   | 15    | 1,500   | 200   | 19.5  | 3,900  |
|         |                   |                                                   | 150   | 19.5  | 2,925   | 50    | 19.5  | 975    |
| 16-5-X2 | 500   | 21.9 | 10,950    |                                           |                 | 500   | 21.9  | 10,950 |
| 21-5-X2 |                   |                                                   | 100   | 19.5  | 1,950   | 50    | 19.5  | 975    |
| 25-5-X2 |                   |                                                   | 50    | 19.5  | 975     | 500   | 21.9  | 10,950 |
|         |                   |                                                   | 400   | 21.9  | 8,760   | 100   | 21.9  | 2,190  |

Therefore, the value of Inventory as on 31-5-20X2 will be as follows: Value of Inventory as per FIFO Method:  
100 units @ ₹ 21.9 = ₹ 2,190  

Working Note:  
(i) Per unit cost of raw material purchased on May 2,20X2 = \(\frac{300 \times 18 + 300 + 150}{300}\) = ₹19.5  
(ii) Per unit cost of raw material purchased on May 16,20X2 = \(\frac{500 \times 21 + 300 + 150}{500}\) = ₹ 21.9  

Note:  
(a) Freight and unloading charges are directly attributable cost and are necessary to bring the inventory into present location and condition hence are included in the cost of inventory.  
(b) Warehouse rent are indirectly attributable expenses and are not considered as cost of inventory.  
(c) Administrative expenses are indirectly attributable expenses and do not usually add any specific value to inventories and hence excluded from the cost of inventory.
question_cleaned:
The following details are available of raw material of a manufacturing unit:

| Date     | Particulars            | Units | Rate per unit |
|----------|------------------------|-------|---------------|
| 1-5-20X2 | Opening Inventory      | 100   | @ ₹ 15 per unit |
| 2-5-20X2 | Purchases              | 300   | @ ₹ 18 per unit |
| 5-5-20X2 | Issued for consumption | 250   | units          |
| 16-5-20X2| Purchases              | 500   | @ ₹ 21 per unit |
| 21-5-20X2| Issued for consumption | 100   | units          |
| 25-5-20X2| Issued for consumption | 450   | units          |

The manufacturer also incurred the following expenses:
- Freight ₹ 300 and unloading charges of ₹ 150 at the time of every purchase respectively.
- Warehouse rent of ₹ 2,000 per month.



- Administrative Expenses of ₹ 1,500 per month.  
You are required to find out the value of inventory as on May 31, 20X2 if the company follows:  
(a) Weighted Average method for inventory valuation.  
(b) First in First Out method for inventory valuation.

question:
Manish closed his books of account on 31st March, each year. Inventory taking for the year ended 31st March, 20X2 was completed by 10th April, 20X2 on which date value of the stock available in godown was of ₹4,50,000 at cost.

Following are the details of transactions that took place between 31st March, 20X2 and 10th April, 20X2:
(i)   Goods sold to customers ₹1,10,000.
(ii)  Sales return ₹ 10,000.
(iii) Purchases ₹ 85,000 (Including Cash Purchases ₹ 10,000).
(iv) Purchases return amounted to ₹ 2,500.
(v)  Goods costing 15,000 received in March, for sale on consignment basis, out of which 60% of goods had been sold by 10th April. These sales are not included in above sales.
(vi) After the stock was taken, it was found that there was certain very old slow- moving items costing ₹ 14,850, which should be taken at ₹ 9,500 to ensure disposal to an interested customer.
Goods are sold at a profit margin of 25% on cost. Ascertain the value of inventory for inclusion in the final accounts for the year ended 31st March, 20X2.
answer:
| Statement of Valuation of Inventory as on 31st March, 20X2 |
|------------------------------------------------------------|
| Particulars                                                | Amount (₹) | Amount (₹) |
|------------------------------------------------------------|------------|------------|
| Value of stock as on 10th April, 20X2                      |            | 4,50,000   |
| Add: Cost of sales during the period from 31st March, 20X2 to 10th April, 20X2: Sales (₹ 1,10,000-₹ 10,000) | 1,00,000   |            |
| Less: Gross profit (25% on cost i.e. 20% on sales)         | (20,000)   | 80,000     |
|                                                            |            | 5,30,000   |
| Less: Purchases during the period from 31st March, 20X2 to 10th April, 20X2 (85,000- 2,500) | 82,500     |            |
| Unsold stock out of goods received on consignment basis (40% of ₹ 15,000) | 6,000      |            |
| Loss on revaluation of slow-moving inventories (14,850-9,500) | 5,350      | 93,850     |
|                                                            |            | 4,36,150   |

| ALTERNATE PRESENTATION                                    |
|------------------------------------------------------------|
| Statement of Valuation of Inventory as on 31st March, 20X2 |
|------------------------------------------------------------|
| Particulars                                                | Amount (₹) | Amount (₹) |
|------------------------------------------------------------|------------|------------|
| Value of stock as on 10th April, 20X2                      |            | 4,50,000   |
| Add: Cost of sales during the period from 31st March, 20X2 to 10th April, 20X2: Sales | 1,10,000   |            |
| Less: Gross profit (25% on cost i.e. 20% on sales)         | (22,000)   | 88,000     |
| Purchase Returns                                           | 2,500      |            |
|                                                            |            | 5,40,500   |
| Less: Purchases during the period from 31st March, 20X2 to 10th April, 20X2: | 85,000     |            |
| Sales Returns                                              | 8,000      |            |
| Unsold stock out of goods received on consignment basis (40% of ₹ 15,000) | 6,000      |            |
| Loss on revaluation of slow-moving inventories (14,850-9,500) | 5,350      | 1,04,350   |
|                                                            |            | 4,36,150   |
question_cleaned:
Manish closed his books of account on 31st March, each year. Inventory taking for the year ended 31st March, 20X2 was completed by 10th April, 20X2 on which date value of the stock available in godown was of ₹4,50,000 at cost.

Following are the details of transactions that took place between 31st March, 20X2 and 10th April, 20X2:
(i)   Goods sold to customers ₹1,10,000.
(ii)  Sales return ₹ 10,000.
(iii) Purchases ₹ 85,000 (Including Cash Purchases ₹ 10,000).
(iv) Purchases return amounted to ₹ 2,500.
(v)  Goods costing 15,000 received in March, for sale on consignment basis, out of which 60% of goods had been sold by 10th April. These sales are not included in above sales.
(vi) After the stock was taken, it was found that there was certain very old slow- moving items costing ₹ 14,850, which should be taken at ₹ 9,500 to ensure disposal to an interested customer.
Goods are sold at a profit margin of 25% on cost. Ascertain the value of inventory for inclusion in the final accounts for the year ended 31st March, 20X2.

question: Distinguish between: FIFO and weighted average price method of stock costing.
answer: Under the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) method of valuation of stock, the actual issue of goods is usually the earliest lot on hand. Hence, the stock in hand will therefore consist of the latest consignments. The closing stock is valued at the price paid for such consignments.  The weighted average price method is not a simple average price method. Under this method of valuation of stock, a stock ledger is maintained, recording receipts and issues on daily basis. A new average would be calculated on receiving fresh consignment. The average price thus calculated after considering arrival of new consignment with the previous value of stock and dividing the preceding stock value and the cost of new arrival with the total units of preceding and new arrival will give the weighted average price.
question_cleaned: Distinguish between: FIFO and weighted average price method of stock costing.

question:
From the following information, calculate the historical cost of closing inventories using adjusted selling price method:  
Purchase during the year-₹ 10,00,000  
Sales during the year- ₹ 15,00,000  
Opening Inventory - Nil  
Closing Inventory at selling price - ₹ 2,00,000
answer:
Sales 15,00,000  
Add: Closing inventory (at selling price) 2,00,000  
Selling price of goods available for sale: 17,00,000  
Less: Cost of goods available for sale 10,00,000  
Gross margin 7,00,000  

Rate of gross margin = \(\frac{7,00,000}{17,00,000} \times 100 = 41.18\%\)  
Cost of closing inventory = 2,00,000 less 41.18% of ₹ 2,00,000 = ₹ 1,17,640  
*This rate may also be considered as 41.176% in that case, the closing inventory will be valued at ₹ 1,17,640 OR as 41.17% in that case, the closing inventory will be valued at ₹ 1,17,640
question_cleaned:
From the following information, calculate the historical cost of closing inventories using adjusted selling price method:  
Purchase during the year-₹ 10,00,000  
Sales during the year- ₹ 15,00,000  
Opening Inventory - Nil  
Closing Inventory at selling price - ₹ 2,00,000
